whatItDoes: Validate and parse International Bank Account Numbers (IBANs) using the Mod-97 checksum algorithm. Extracts country code, check digits, BBAN, and validates against 35+ supported country formats with example patterns.
whyItExists: IBAN validation is critical for financial applications and this tool provides instant validation with detailed feedback on the IBAN structure, including country identification and expected format examples.
whoShouldUse: Financial developers, payment processing teams, banking professionals, and anyone who needs to validate or understand IBAN structure for European and international bank accounts.

faq: What is an IBAN? — An International Bank Account Number is a standardized format for identifying bank accounts across borders, used primarily in Europe and parts of the Middle East and Caribbean.

How does Mod-97 validation work? — The IBAN is rearranged (BBAN + country code + check digits), letters are converted to numbers, and the result is divided by 97. If the remainder is 1, the IBAN is valid.
How many countries support IBAN? — This tool supports 35+ countries. IBAN is primarily used in Europe, the Middle East, and some Caribbean nations.
Is this tool sufficient for production use? — For production financial applications, use a dedicated IBAN validation library that handles all edge cases and is regularly updated.
